prompt: Turn this spec into a clickable prototype I can show the office staff tomorrow. source: ./dispatch-spec.md
assertions: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
All four screens from the spec exist: intake queue, dispatch board (techs as columns with job cards), job detail, and end-of-day summary
A job can be assigned from the intake queue to a tech and then appears on that tech's board column
Emergency-priority jobs are visually loud (distinct alert/red styling), per the spec's 'unassigned + emergency needs to be LOUD' note
The close-out flow captures the spec's fields: parts used, time on site, and a needs-follow-up flag
The hardcoded data uses the spec's job fields (zone, equipment, priority, status, assigned tech)
A too-long customer name (like the spec's 'Greater Metropolitan Property Management LLC, Building C') is included so overflow handling is visible
An empty intake queue state is reachable and does not look broken, per the spec's 'slow afternoons' note
A loading state and an error message/toast are each reachable by clicking
